channel.title: Linux on Cyber Security & Linux | Murat Kurkoglu - orioninsist
channel.description: Recent content in Linux on Cyber Security & Linux | Murat Kurkoglu - orioninsist

item.title: Linux logrotate: Prevent Disk Full Outages Without Losing Logs
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Learn how Linux logrotate prevents disk full incidents while preserving useful logs for security and troubleshooting, with safe configs and examples.

item.title: Creating a Professional Favicon Pipeline on Arch Linux
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: How I built a clean, reliable favicon workflow using ImageMagick, OptiPNG, and Hugo PaperMod on Arch Linux.

item.title: The Ultimate Sway Config for a Fast and Stable Wayland Setup (2025 Edition)
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: A complete, production-grade Sway configuration optimized for performance, stability, and minimalism on Wayland. Includes best practices, recommended packages, environment variables, and a full ready-to-use config.

item.title: chmod vs chown: The Practical Differences Explained
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Learn the real differences between chmod and chown, how each affects Linux file permissions, and the exact scenarios where they fix permission errors.

item.title: How to Extract Multi-Part ZIP Files on Linux (The Correct Way)
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Learn how to correctly repair, merge, and extract multi-part ZIP files on Linux using zip -FF, with step-by-step commands for Arch Linux, Ubuntu, and Debian. Fix filename not matched, EOF, and other unzip errors fast.

item.title: Bash 'cd' Command: Master Terminal Navigation Now
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Stop feeling lost in the Linux terminal. Master the 'cd' command, relative vs. absolute paths, and the back button trick 'cd -' to navigate like a pro.

item.title: Bash 'ls': How -lah Unlocks Your File System
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Ever felt lost in your terminal? Master the 'ls' command and its essential flags (-l, -a, -h) to finally 'see' your file system clearly.

item.title: Bash 'pwd': Your 'You Are Here' Map in the Terminal
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Ever feel lost in the Linux terminal? Learn why 'pwd' (Print Working Directory) is more than a command—it's the anchor for your mental model.

item.title: Terminal vs. Shell vs. Bash: Clearing Up the Confusion
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Confused by Terminal, Shell, and Bash? This guide clarifies the essential differences, explains the CLI, and shows why Bash became the standard.

item.title: Wayland Broke My Scripts: Meet wtype, Your New xdotool
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Frustrated that xdotool is broken in Wayland? Learn how to use wtype, the modern tool for keyboard and mouse automation in Sway and Hyprland.

item.title: Rofi vs. Wofi vs. Wmenu: The Ultimate Wayland & X11 Guide
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Rofi vs. Wofi vs. Wmenu: Which app launcher is best for your X11 or Wayland setup? A deep-dive comparison of features, configuration, and philosophy.

item.title: Fix Sway's Flaky Keyboard Layout Switching for Good
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Fix flaky Sway keyboard layout switching for good. This guide details a robust fix using Mod4+space and `swaymsg input type:keyboard` for `xkb_layout`.

item.title: What Is the ~/go Folder & Is It Safe to Delete?
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Discovered a mysterious 'go' directory in your Linux home folder? This guide explains what the Go module cache is, why tools like Hugo create it, and how to safely clean it with go clean -modcache to reclaim disk space.

item.title: Stop Linux Auto-Creating a ~/Downloads Folder
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Tired of the Downloads folder reappearing on your minimalist Arch Linux + Sway setup? This guide shows how to edit the XDG user-dirs.dirs file to permanently set your home directory as the default.

item.title: My Simple Pacman.conf for a Better Arch Experience
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Tired of a bland terminal? Learn how to transform your Arch Linux pacman experience with two simple edits in pacman.conf for a colorful, readable, and faster setup.

item.title: Arch/Sway: Manually Hacking the GTK File Chooser for Ultimate Workflow Speed
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Learn to manually configure the GTK File Chooser Dialog's bookmarks and settings in Arch Linux and Sway. Ditch the visual tools for ultimate workflow efficiency.

item.title: Fedora Sway Spin vs. Arch: The True Cost of Wayland Control
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: I compared the ready-to-use Fedora Sway Spin with my custom Arch Sway setup. Discover the critical differences in stability, performance, and the cost of total control.

item.title: Digital Chaos to Zen Clarity: How I Rebuilt My Life with the P.A.R.A. Method
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Overwhelmed by digital clutter? Discover how I used the P.A.R.A. method on Arch Linux to build a 'Second Brain' and achieve zen-like organization.

item.title: Forget Your Desktop: The Philosophy of My Minimalist Arch + Sway System
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Journey from a bloated desktop user to the architect of a minimalist Arch Linux and Sway system. Discover the philosophy of taking back control.

item.title: How to Install Docker Desktop on Debian
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description:
Docker is a powerful platform that allows you to develop, ship, and run applications inside containers. Installing Docker on Debian is a straightforward process, and in this guide, we’ll walk you through the steps to get Docker Desktop up and running on your Debian-based system.

  • Prerequisites Before we begin, make sure you have the following prerequisites in place:

A Debian-based system (such as Debian itself or Ubuntu) Administrative (sudo) privileges

item.title: Bash Scripting Code and Automation
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Enhance your coding skills and automate tasks with Bash scripting. Learn Bash commands and boost productivity.

item.title: Linux Mastery Essential Commands Shortcuts
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Master Linux commands, shortcuts, and permissions for effective Linux usage

item.title: Linux Essentials Mastering Key Commands
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Explore vital Linux commands like 'cd,' 'ls,' 'file,' and more, plus valuable tips and shortcuts. Perfect for beginners mastering Linux commands.

item.title: Mastering Essential Linux Commands
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Learn Linux essentials: cp, mv, clear, and more. Boost your productivity with these vital Linux commands.

item.title: Linux Command Line Basics
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Learn essential Linux commands: ls, grep, cd, man, and more in this comprehensive guide

item.title: Essential Linux Terminal Commands and Examples
item.author: muratkurkoglu@gmail.com (Murat Kurkoglu)
item.description: Master essential Linux terminal commands with explanations and practical examples. Learn how to navigate, copy, delete files, and more. Enhance your command-line skills now!
